name: Canterbury Jumbles
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. In a large mixing bowl, beat together the shortening, butter and brown sugar until smooth.
3. Beat in the eggs, then stir in the buttermilk and vanilla.
4. Blend the flour, baking soda and salt in a small bowl.
5. Stir the flour mixture into the butter mixture until incorporated.
6. Stir in the coconut, nuts, raisins and chocolate chips.
7. Drop by spoonfuls onto greased cookie sheets.
8. Bake for 7-10 minutes, until the cookies are puffed and slightly brown.
9. Cool on a rack.
10. Store in an airtight tin.
